nodes.label: AI as knowledge resource for unfamiliar domains
nodes.label_negative: AI as integrated system for routine tasks
nodes.grounding_span: It is a window that I have open that I refer to when I have questions about something strange

nodes.label: AI reliability for practical tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I wouldn't fully automate until I am very confident in it becuase of the implications of something going wrong

nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner vs delegator
nodes.grounding_span: I tend to collaborate. But that is a knowledge gap

nodes.label: AI adoption in workplace
nodes.grounding_span: our work culture is backwards with the main core group not open to change

nodes.label: AI as skill development enabler
nodes.label_negative: Human skill retention despite AI capability
nodes.grounding_span: I don't feel a need for that. Those skills would still be there one day in the future if I needed them

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: This meant we were able to send the product without delay

nodes.label: Operational reliability
nodes.grounding_span: loss of confidence by the customer, claims for lost freight, costs to correct the issue

nodes.label: Personal growth and knowledge
nodes.grounding_span: I just need to learn how to do it

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as a helpful tool
Caution toward AI in high-risk tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I wouldn't fully automate until I am very confident in it

nodes.label: Optimism about future AI integration
nodes.grounding_span: I hope that it will evolve hugely and take over email monitoring and truck planning

nodes.label: Frustration with organizational and IT barriers
nodes.grounding_span: IT access is very restricted so we can be blocked by what we add to the computer

nodes.label: Pragmatic cost-benefit evaluation
nodes.grounding_span: If AI can do a tsk better and more efficiently than me and I can use it to improve my life, then why wouldn't I?

nodes.label: Learning-by-doing approach
nodes.grounding_span: I tend to collaborate. But that is a knowledge gap. When I learn to use it fully at work then I would be happy to leave it to complete a task
